title: dubautia laxa subsp. pseudoplantaginea
intro: Dubautia laxa subsp. pseudoplantaginea is a rare and lesser-known species of flowering plant, belonging to the Asteraceae family, native to the Hawaiian Islands. This plant, also known as 'ohia lehua, has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, derived from its leaves and flowers. The health benefits of Dubautia laxa subsp. pseudoplantaginea include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Dubautia laxa subsp. pseudoplantaginea can be prepared as a herbal tea, infused oil, or salve, showcasing the versatility of its medicinal applications.
